label: Contract Draft
description: Draft a clear, balanced commercial agreement from your deal terms

fields.label: Parties
fields.placeholder: Acme Inc. (Provider) and Beta LLC (Customer)

fields.label: Deal terms & scope
fields.placeholder: Acme provides analytics software; 12-month term; $5k/mo; net-30 payment.

fields.label: Governing law
fields.placeholder: State of Delaware, USA

fields.label: Term & termination
fields.placeholder: 12 months, auto-renew, 30-day notice to terminate

fields.label: Other instructions
fields.placeholder: Cap liability at fees paid; exclude consequential damages; US jurisdiction only.

template:
You are a senior commercial contracts attorney. Draft a {{contractType}} between {{parties}}.

## Deal terms
{{purpose}}

## Structure
- Recitals, definitions, then numbered operative clauses, signature block, and any schedules/exhibits.
{{#if term}}- Term & termination: {{term}}.
{{/if}}{{#if governingLaw}}- Governing law and venue: {{governingLaw}}.
{{/if}}{{#if clauses}}- Include and fully draft these clauses: {{clauses}}.
{{/if}}- Drafting posture: {{#if favor == "Balanced / mutual"}}mutual and even-handed.{{/if}}{{#if favor == "Favor first party"}}protective of the first-named party while remaining commercially reasonable.{{/if}}{{#if favor == "Favor second party"}}protective of the second-named party while remaining commercially reasonable.{{/if}}

## Drafting standards
- Precise defined terms used consistently; no undefined capitalized terms.
{{#if plainLanguage}}- Plain, readable language; short sentences; avoid archaic legalese where a clearer term works.
{{/if}}- Flag any open business decisions with a clearly marked [BRACKETED] placeholder rather than guessing.
{{#if constraints}}
## Specific instructions
{{constraints}}
{{/if}}
## Deliverables
1. The complete draft agreement, ready to populate.
2. A short bullet list of assumptions made and decision points to confirm.

This is a draft for review by a qualified lawyer and is not legal advice. Proceed with well-reasoned, clearly-flagged defaults; ask only if a term is genuinely blocking.

label: Contract Review
description: Get a risk-flagged review and redline of an agreement you received

fields.label: Contract text
fields.placeholder: Paste the full agreement or the clauses you want reviewed.

fields.label: Deal context
fields.placeholder: $120k/yr SaaS deal, our data is sensitive, hard go-live date

template:
You are an experienced contracts counsel reviewing an agreement on behalf of the {{role}}.

## Agreement under review
{{contractText}}

{{#if context}}## Deal context
{{context}}

{{/if}}## Review focus
{{#if concerns}}- Prioritize: {{concerns}}.
{{/if}}- Risk appetite: {{riskAppetite}}.

## What to produce
{{#if summary}}- A plain-English summary of what the {{role}} is agreeing to.
{{/if}}- A risk register: each issue with severity (High / Medium / Low), the clause reference, why it matters to the {{role}}, and a recommended position.
{{#if redline}}- Concrete suggested redline language for High and Medium issues.
{{/if}}- Any missing protective clauses the {{role}} should request.

## Deliverables
1. Summary{{#if summary}} and{{/if}} prioritized risk register.
2. Recommended negotiation asks, ranked by leverage and importance.

This is a draft review for confirmation by a qualified lawyer and is not legal advice. Be specific and cite clause numbers; do not speculate beyond the text provided.

label: Privacy Policy
description: Generate a privacy policy tailored to how your product handles data

fields.label: Company / product name
fields.placeholder: Acme Analytics

fields.label: What data you collect & why
fields.placeholder: Account email, usage analytics, support messages; used to provide and improve the service.

fields.label: Third parties / sub-processors
fields.placeholder: Stripe (payments), AWS (hosting), Segment (analytics), Intercom (support).

fields.label: Privacy contact
fields.placeholder: privacy@acme.com

fields.label: Sells or shares data for ads

template:
You are a privacy counsel specializing in data protection compliance. Draft a privacy policy for {{company}} written for a {{audience}} reader.

## Data practices
{{dataPractices}}

## Compliance scope
{{#if jurisdictions}}- Must satisfy: {{jurisdictions}}. Include the rights and disclosures each regime requires.
{{/if}}{{#if thirdParties}}- Third parties / sub-processors: {{thirdParties}}.
{{/if}}{{#if cookies}}- Cover cookies and tracking technologies and how users can control them.
{{/if}}{{#if selling}}- Disclose data sharing/sale for advertising and the opt-out mechanism (incl. "Do Not Sell or Share").
{{else}}- State clearly that personal data is not sold or shared for cross-context behavioral advertising.
{{/if}}
## Required sections
- Data collected and purposes, legal bases, retention, security, international transfers, user rights and how to exercise them, children's data, changes to the policy, and contact{{#if contact}} ({{contact}}){{/if}}.

## Deliverables
1. The complete, well-organized policy with clear headings and an "effective date" placeholder.
2. A short list of facts the company must verify or fill in before publishing.

This is a draft for review by a qualified lawyer and is not legal advice. Use plain language, mark any unknowns as [BRACKETED] placeholders, and do not invent practices not described above.

label: Terms of Service
description: Draft terms of service covering use, payment, liability and termination

fields.label: Company / product name
fields.placeholder: Acme Analytics

fields.label: What the service does & how it's used
fields.placeholder: A web analytics dashboard offered on monthly and annual subscription tiers.

fields.label: Governing law
fields.placeholder: State of Delaware, USA

fields.label: Minimum user age
fields.placeholder: 18 (or 13 with consent)

template:
You are a technology transactions attorney. Draft Terms of Service for {{company}}.

## The service
{{serviceDescription}}
- Commercial model: {{model}}.
{{#if minAge}}- Minimum age to use the service: {{minAge}}.
{{/if}}
## Provisions to draft
{{#if policies}}- Include fully drafted sections for: {{policies}}.
{{/if}}{{#if arbitration}}- Include a binding individual-arbitration clause and class-action waiver, with a clearly described opt-out.
{{/if}}{{#if governingLaw}}- Governing law and venue: {{governingLaw}}.
{{/if}}- Standard sections: acceptance, account terms, user obligations, termination/suspension, disclaimers, limitation of liability, indemnification, changes to terms, and contact.

## Drafting standards
- Numbered sections with clear headings; defined terms used consistently.
- Enforceable, readable language; conspicuous formatting for disclaimers and liability limits.
- Mark any business specifics as [BRACKETED] placeholders rather than inventing them.

## Deliverables
1. The complete Terms of Service with an "effective date" placeholder.
2. A short list of items the company must confirm before going live.

This is a draft for review by a qualified lawyer and is not legal advice. Proceed with well-reasoned defaults and flag open points clearly.

label: Clause Explainer
description: Explain what a contract clause means and its practical risks

fields.label: Clause text
fields.placeholder: Paste the exact clause or provision you want explained.

options.label: The party being asked to sign

fields.label: Surrounding context
fields.placeholder: It's in a SaaS vendor agreement; we'd be the customer.

template:
You are a contracts lawyer who is excellent at explaining legal language to a {{audience}}. Explain the following clause from the perspective of {{perspective}}.

## Clause
{{clause}}

{{#if context}}## Context
{{context}}

{{/if}}## Explain
- Plain-English meaning, sentence by sentence where it helps.
- What it obligates or protects, and the practical consequences if things go wrong.
- The realistic risks and any red flags for {{perspective}}.
{{#if examples}}- A concrete worked example showing how the clause would play out.
{{/if}}{{#if alternatives}}- Fairer or more balanced alternative wording, with a one-line rationale for each change.
{{/if}}
## Deliverables
1. The explanation, structured and jargon-free.
2. A one-sentence "bottom line" verdict on whether this clause is standard, aggressive, or unusual.

This is an educational explanation for review by a qualified lawyer and is not legal advice. Stay strictly grounded in the clause text provided; note where context is needed rather than assuming.

label: Compliance Checklist
description: Build an actionable compliance checklist for a regulation or framework

fields.label: Regulation / framework
fields.placeholder: GDPR, SOC 2 Type II, HIPAA, PCI-DSS, ISO 27001

fields.label: Business & data context
fields.placeholder: B2B SaaS, 40 staff, EU + US customers, stores customer PII in AWS.

template:
You are a compliance and information-governance specialist. Build a practical compliance checklist for {{framework}}.

## Organization context
{{business}}
- Current maturity: {{stage}}.
{{#if domains}}- Cover these domains: {{domains}}.
{{/if}}
## Checklist requirements
- Organize by domain; each item is a concrete, verifiable control written as an action.
{{#if owners}}- For each item include a suggested owner role and a priority (Critical / High / Medium).
{{/if}}{{#if evidence}}- For each item note the evidence or artifact that would demonstrate compliance.
{{/if}}- Highlight the gaps most likely to fail an audit given the stated maturity.

## Deliverables
1. The structured checklist, grouped by domain.
2. A prioritized "first 30 days" shortlist of the highest-impact items.

This is a draft for review by a qualified compliance professional or lawyer and is not legal advice. Tie items to the named framework's actual requirements; flag where a formal mapping or external assessment is required.

label: Demand Letter
description: Draft a firm, professional demand letter setting out your claim

fields.label: Sender
fields.placeholder: Acme Inc., 123 Main St

fields.label: Recipient
fields.placeholder: Beta LLC, attn: Accounts Payable

fields.label: Facts & background
fields.placeholder: Invoice #482 for $18,400 issued 2026-01-10, due net-30, unpaid despite 3 reminders.

fields.label: Remedy demanded
fields.placeholder: Payment of $18,400 plus late fees within 14 days

fields.label: Response deadline
fields.placeholder: 14 days from the date of this letter

template:
You are a litigation attorney drafting a pre-action demand letter. Write a {{tone}} demand letter from {{sender}} to {{recipient}}.

## Nature of claim
{{claimType}}

## Facts
{{facts}}

## Demand
- Clearly state the remedy demanded: {{remedy}}.
{{#if deadline}}- Set a firm response deadline: {{deadline}}.
{{/if}}{{#if escalation}}- State, professionally and without bluffing, the next steps the sender is prepared to take if the demand is not met by the deadline.
{{/if}}
## Letter requirements
- Formal letter format: date, parties, RE: line, numbered factual background, the demand, deadline, and a closing.
- Assertive but measured; factual, not emotional or threatening; no admissions.
- Reserve all rights and remedies. Mark any unknown details as [BRACKETED] placeholders.

## Deliverables
1. The complete, send-ready letter.
2. A short note of facts or documents the sender should attach or verify.

This is a draft for review by a qualified lawyer and is not legal advice. Do not assert legal conclusions or statute citations unless clearly supported by the facts given.

label: Non-Disclosure Agreement
description: Mutual or one-way NDA tailored to the disclosure context.

fields.label: Disclosing / first party
fields.placeholder: Northwind Inc.

fields.label: Receiving / second party
fields.placeholder: Acme LLC

fields.label: Purpose of disclosure
fields.placeholder: Evaluating a potential integration partnership and data-sharing pilot.

fields.label: What counts as confidential
fields.placeholder: Source code, roadmaps, pricing, customer data; excludes public info.

fields.label: Governing law / jurisdiction
fields.placeholder: State of Delaware, USA

template:
You are a senior commercial contracts attorney. Draft a {{ndaType}} Non-Disclosure Agreement between {{partyA}} and {{partyB}}.

## Purpose
{{purpose}}

## Drafting requirements
- Structure: parties, definitions, confidentiality obligations, exclusions, term, remedies, miscellaneous.
{{#if confScope}}- Confidential Information scope: {{confScope}}
{{/if}}- Standard exclusions: publicly available, independently developed, rightfully received, legally compelled.
- Confidentiality term: {{term}}{{#if term == "perpetual"}} (perpetual specifically for trade secrets; fixed term for other confidential info){{/if}}.
{{#if ndaType == "mutual"}}- Make obligations reciprocal and balanced for both parties.
{{else}}- Make obligations run primarily to the receiving party.
{{/if}}{{#if includeReturn}}- Include a return-or-destruction-on-request clause.
{{/if}}{{#if governingLaw}}- Governing law and venue: {{governingLaw}}.
{{/if}}- Use clear defined terms; flag any [BRACKETED] items needing business input.

## Deliverables
1. The full NDA draft.
2. A plain-English summary of key obligations and risks.
3. A list of open points to confirm with the business.

This is a draft for review by a qualified lawyer and does not constitute legal advice.

label: Data Processing Addendum
description: GDPR/CCPA-aligned DPA between controller and processor.

fields.label: Controller (customer)
fields.placeholder: Acme LLC

fields.label: Processor (vendor)
fields.placeholder: Northwind Cloud Inc.

fields.label: Service & processing context
fields.placeholder: SaaS analytics platform processing customer end-user account and usage data.

fields.label: Categories of personal data
fields.placeholder: Names, emails, IP addresses, device IDs, support messages.

template:
You are a senior data privacy counsel. Draft a Data Processing Addendum where {{controller}} is the controller and {{processor}} is the processor.

## Processing context
{{service}}

## Personal data categories
{{dataTypes}}

## Drafting requirements
- Align with: {{regimes}}. Define roles, processing purpose, duration, and data subject categories.
- Cover: processor obligations, confidentiality, security measures, data subject rights assistance, audit rights, deletion/return on termination.
{{#if subprocessors}}- Include sub-processor authorization, flow-down obligations, and a change-notification mechanism.
{{else}}- Prohibit sub-processors without prior written consent.
{{/if}}{{#if transfers}}- Address international transfers (e.g., SCCs / transfer mechanism) and a transfer impact note.
{{/if}}- Personal data breach notification: {{breachWindow}}, with required content of the notice.
- Reference a Security Measures schedule and a Processing Details schedule; flag [BRACKETED] inputs.

## Deliverables
1. The full DPA draft with schedules outlined.
2. A plain-English summary of obligations and residual risks.
3. Open points to confirm with the business and security teams.

This is a draft for review by a qualified lawyer and does not constitute legal advice.

label: Cease and Desist Letter
description: Firm cease-and-desist letter demanding a party stop infringing conduct.

fields.label: Sender / rights holder
fields.placeholder: Northwind Inc.

fields.label: Recipient
fields.placeholder: Copycat Co.

fields.label: Facts & evidence of the conduct
fields.placeholder: Recipient launched a product using our registered mark 'Northwind' on their homepage since March.

fields.label: Rights / basis being asserted
fields.placeholder: USPTO Reg. No. 1234567 for 'Northwind' in class 9, in continuous use since 2016.

fields.label: Specific demands
fields.placeholder: Stop all use, remove from website and app stores, written confirmation within 14 days.

fields.label: Response deadline
fields.placeholder: 14 days from the date of this letter

fields.label: Reserve all legal rights / litigation notice

template:
You are a litigation attorney drafting demand correspondence. Write a cease and desist letter from {{sender}} to {{recipient}} regarding {{issueType}}.

## Facts
{{facts}}

## Rights asserted
{{rights}}

## Demands
{{demands}}

## Drafting requirements
- Professional letterhead-style format: date, recipient, RE: line, body, signature block.
- State the facts and the legal basis precisely; avoid exaggeration or unprovable claims.
- Make the demands numbered, specific, and verifiable.
- Set a clear response deadline: {{#if deadline}}{{deadline}}{{else}}a specified reasonable period{{/if}}.
{{#if reserveRights}}- Include an express reservation of all rights and notice that failure to comply may lead to legal action, without making threats that could constitute unlawful intimidation.
{{/if}}- Keep tone firm but measured; flag [BRACKETED] items needing confirmation.

## Deliverables
1. The full cease and desist letter.
2. A plain-English summary of the position and risks.
3. Open points and evidence to confirm before sending.

This is a draft for review by a qualified lawyer and does not constitute legal advice.
